title: Plugin manifest
description: Unified plugin manifest schema, returned by GET {base_url}/manifest (docs/plugin-system/README.md §5.1, docs/plugin-system/phase1-implementation.md §4). A single document type supports every wire contract_version a plugin advertises via supported_contract_versions: extension points opt in to contract_version 2's deferred completion protocol individually (completion_mode: deferred), everything else about this document is contract_version-agnostic. There is no manifest-level contract_version field: contract negotiation is expressed entirely by the supported_contract_versions array. This schema is domain-neutral: it describes generic plugin metadata and extension-point wiring only, with no courseware- or content-specific fields; extension_points[].type is a fixed, generic vocabulary (currently exactly one published value, review-export-action), never a hard-coded action_id or vendor name.

manifest_version.description: Fixed document-format version for this manifest schema itself (distinct from the wire contract_version(s) a plugin's actions may support, see supported_contract_versions). docs/plugin-system/phase1-implementation.md §4.2: 'manifest_version == 1'.

version.description: The plugin's own release version, independent of contract_version.

license.description: Human-readable license identifier/name (e.g. an SPDX identifier, or 'Proprietary').

source_url.description: Public source code location, required by hosted-channel policy for copyleft-licensed plugins (out of scope for this repository to enforce); null when not applicable/not published.

type.description: http-remote: the plugin is an independently deployed HTTP service reachable at an out-of-band-registered base_url (the only channel this repository's OpenAPI documents describe). oci/zip/wasm: hosted-artifact runtime forms (docs/plugin-system/README.md §5.4/§5.1); their additional packaging fields are intentionally not standardized by this schema yet.

supported_contract_versions.description: Wire contract_version(s) this plugin supports, for compatibility negotiation at registration time (docs/plugin-system/README.md §5.1: '契约协商——平台登记时校验与自身支持版本的交集，无交集即拒绝安装'). Must include 2 if any extension_points[] entry declares completion_mode: deferred.

invoke.description: Path (not an absolute URL), rooted at the plugin's base_url, that the host POSTs to (with a further '/{action_id}' segment appended) to invoke an extension point. Documented concrete value: '/actions' (docs/plugin-system/README.md §5.1), yielding POST {base_url}/actions/{action_id}.

operation_status.description: Path template (not an absolute URL), rooted at the plugin's base_url, that the host GETs to poll a deferred operation's status. Documented concrete value: '/operations/{operation_id}'. Required if and only if at least one extension_points[] entry declares completion_mode: deferred.

endpoints.description: Top-level, path-based endpoint advertisements. Always relative to the plugin's own base_url (established out of band at registration, see openapi/plugin-api.yaml servers block); never absolute URLs.

scopes.description: Closed set of permission scopes this plugin requests (docs/plugin-system/phase1-implementation.md §4.2: 'scopes ⊆ {tree:export:read, user:identity:basic, user:contact:mobile}'). Gates which optional invoke-request fields (docs/protocol.md) the host will populate.

extension_points.description: Declared extension points. Phase 1 publishes exactly one generic extension point type, review-export-action (docs/plugin-system/README.md §5.2); it is not courseware- or vendor-specific.

type.description: Fixed, published, generic extension-point type. Not a domain-specific vocabulary item: 'review-export-action' names a UI/interaction shape (an action button on the review/export surface), not any particular partner or content domain (docs/plugin-system/README.md §5.2).

label.description: Human-readable button/action label, rendered as escaped plain text only (docs/plugin-system/README.md §5.2: hosts never render plugin-supplied HTML/JS).

icon.description: Short plain-text glyph/emoji, not an image URL or markup.

confirm_text.description: Optional confirmation-dialog plain text shown before invoking this extension point.

timeout_seconds.description: Per-invocation timeout ceiling; the host applies min(timeout_seconds, its own hard cap) (docs/plugin-system/phase1-implementation.md §6.3).

completion_mode.description: sync (default): the plugin's invoke response is the final result (v1 envelope, see v1/invoke-response.schema.json). deferred: the plugin's invoke response only accepts the request (v2 accepted envelope, see v2/invoke-accepted-response.schema.json); the final result arrives later via a completion event and/or operation status polling. A manifest with any deferred extension point MUST list 2 in supported_contract_versions and MUST declare endpoints.operation_status.

interaction_mode.description: none (default): no browser handoff is required. launch_url: the end user must continue this operation in a browser context reachable via a launch_url supplied on the v2 invoke-accepted response. This property never appears on the invoke-accepted response body itself (see v2/invoke-accepted-response.schema.json); the host enforces launch_url's presence/absence there by cross-referencing this manifest declaration for the invoked action_id.

format.description: Serialization format of the exported tree payload. Documented value: 'json'.

schema_version.description: Fixed version of the exported tree's own internal schema (docs/plugin-system/phase1-implementation.md §4.2: 'input_contract.schema_version == 1').

include_skin_html.description: When true, the invoke request's tree.nodes[*] entries additionally carry id/depth and (when a rendered skin artifact exists) skin_html; the top-level skin object is populated (or explicitly null on silent degrade, never a validation failure) — docs/plugin-system/phase1-implementation.md §6.2.

inputContract.description: Declares which optional, potentially large/sensitive fields of the exported tree this extension point wants inlined into its invoke request (data minimization).
